description: Three-way performance comparison: will the S&P 500 outperform both Bitcoin and Gold in 2026? Least favored at 20%, reflecting equity underperformance amid tariff uncertainty and recession risk. Uses price return only — excludes dividends.
question: Will the S&P 500 have the best performance in 2026 (vs Bitcoin and Gold)?

resolution_logic.summary: Resolves Yes if S&P 500's percentage price change in 2026 exceeds both Bitcoin's and Gold's. Uses Yahoo Finance ^SPX close prices for last trading day of 2025 vs last trading day of 2026.

triggers.description: S&P 500 YTD return exceeds both Bitcoin and Gold YTD returns
triggers.metric: S&P 500 Index (^SPX) % change (Yahoo Finance close, last trading day 2025 vs last trading day 2026)
triggers.threshold: Greater than both BTC (Binance) and Gold (MarketWatch GC00)

exclusions: Total return (including dividends) — only price return
S&P 500 Total Return Index — must be price-only ^SPX
resolution_logic.ambiguity_notes: S&P 500 uses price return only, not total return — excludes ~1.3% annual dividend yield. This slightly disadvantages S&P vs assets with no yield (BTC) since dividends aren't counted. Yahoo Finance is the authoritative source — other data providers' ^SPX values may differ slightly.

catalyst_dates.event: Q1 2026 earnings season begins — corporate profit signals
April FOMC — rate path impacts equity valuations
US midterm elections — policy uncertainty catalyst

cross_platform_link.structural_notes: No equivalent on Kalshi. Coverage gap.

related_markets.notes: Gold best performer at 54% — current favorite. S&P at 20% is the least favored of the three.
Bitcoin best performer at 30% — second place. Implied market ranking: Gold > BTC > S&P 500.
